purpose: Convert goal ambiguity into the smallest deterministic question batch before planning or implementation.

question_rules.requirement: Each question unlocks or blocks exactly one declared gate.
Each question maps to one versioned operator from the executable question calculus.
Ask only for information that changes the next permitted action.
The answer type is explicit and machine-checked.
Allowed answer shape, valid answer strings, and invalid answer strings are present.
Required proof source is declared before the answer can unlock a gate.
Scope expansion is rejected unless scope_change_request is true.
Semantically equivalent active questions share one stable fingerprint and only one emitted question remains.
Questions are ordered by blocked gate severity, execution risk, and cost of wrong execution.
Each question has a terminal or review state before replay.

priority_policy.meaning: Wrong or missing answer can authorize an unsafe next action.
Wrong answer can waste material work or produce invalid proof.
Wrong answer affects ordering or reporting only.

fail_closed_rules: Reject a question with no gate_id.
Reject a question with no operator_id.
Reject a question with no answer_type.
Reject duplicate question_fingerprint values in one result.
Reject scope-changing question text when scope_change_request is false.
Reject P0 questions when blocks_execution is not true.
Reject any role result containing planning or implementation instructions.

codex_exec_contract.input_policy: Prompt compiler may include only goal packet, current decision graph, known answers, allowed question operators, relevant context pack ids, and role profile.
codex_exec_contract.output_policy: Emit JSON only. Do not emit prose, plans, implementation steps, or commands.
